perturbation.original_text: Schoolpop shall secure a physical location for maintaining, distributing, and fulfilling Card inventory no later than August 1, 2004 which location shall be subject to such security requirements (the "Security Requirements") as shall be required by AEIS or American Express Travel Related Services, Inc. all as further set forth in the Trust Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit 1.
perturbation.changed_text: Schoolpop shall secure a physical location for maintaining, distributing, and fulfilling Card inventory no later than August 1, 2004.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'which location shall be subject to such security requirements (the "Security Requirements") as shall be required by AEIS or American Express Travel Related Services, Inc. all as further set forth in the Trust Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit 1,' the modified text creates an ambiguity regarding the necessity of adhering to specific security standards. Since other sections of the contract, such as the Trust Agreement, still reference Security Requirements. This creates an in-text contradiction as the requirements referred to in the Trust Agreement are not being defined or mentioned in the original location where it should be defined in the current updated section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schoolpop shall secure a physical location for maintaining, distributing, and fulfilling Card inventory no later than August 1, 2004.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the physical location must adhere to 'Security Requirements' defined in the Trust Agreement (Exhibit 1). The changed text omits this requirement, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text still references a physical location, but the modified text removes the obligation for that location to adhere to specific Security Requirements, which directly contradicts the intention of referencing Security Requirements elsewhere in the document like in the Trust Agreement.

perturbation.original_text: In any consecutive three (3) month period in which the difference between the Card Inventory Forecast less the actual Card Order volume for that period is a shortage of ten percent or greater (10%+), AEIS reserves the right to assess a penalty, ("Card Inventory Penalty") of sixteen and one-half cents ($0.165) per Card for such difference as detailed in Examples 1 and 2 below.
perturbation.changed_text: In any consecutive three (3) month period in which the difference between the Card Inventory Forecast less the actual Card Order volume for that period is a shortage of ten percent or greater (10%+), AEIS reserves the right to assess a penalty, ("Card Inventory Penalty") for such difference as detailed in Examples 1 and 2 below.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'of sixteen and one-half cents ($0.165) per Card,' while retaining the examples, creates a contradiction. The examples now lack a defined penalty amount, making the enforcement of the Card Inventory Penalty uncertain as the method for calculation is missing from this clause, creating an in-text contradiction since the penalty is still mentione dbut without its actual definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In any consecutive three (3) month period in which the difference between the Card Inventory Forecast less the actual Card Order volume for that period is a shortage of ten percent or greater (10%+), AEIS reserves the right to assess a penalty, ("Card Inventory Penalty") for such difference as detailed in Examples 1 and 2 below.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific penalty amount ('of sixteen and one-half cents ($0.165) per Card'). The contradicted text, which is the same as the original, refers to 'Examples 1 and 2' that would presumably illustrate the calculation of this penalty. However, without the penalty amount defined in the changed section, the examples become meaningless and unenforceable. This creates an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction', because essential language concerning the calculation is removed, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each policy required hereunder shall provide that AEIS shall receive thirty (30) days advance written notice in the event of a cancellation or material change in such policy. Each policy of insurance which Schoolpop is required to possess under this Agreement shall name AEIS, and its Directors, Officers, and Employees, as additional insured in the insurance policy limits herein required. In the event that any service under this Agreement is to be rendered by persons other than Schoolpop's employees, Schoolpop shall furnish AEIS with evidence of insurance for such persons subject to the same terms and conditions as set forth above and applicable to Schoolpop prior to commencement of service by such person(s).
perturbation.changed_text: Each policy required hereunder shall provide that AEIS shall receive thirty (30) days advance written notice in the event of a cancellation or material change in such policy. In the event that any service under this Agreement is to be rendered by persons other than Schoolpop's employees, Schoolpop shall furnish AEIS with evidence of insurance for such persons subject to the same terms and conditions as set forth above and applicable to Schoolpop prior to commencement of service by such person(s).
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'Each policy of insurance which Schoolpop is required to possess under this Agreement shall name AEIS, and its Directors, Officers, and Employees, as additional insured in the insurance policy limits herein required.' creates an internal contradiction. Other sections of the agreement still allude to the need for specific insurance coverage that benefits AEIS. This creates uncertainty in enforcement. If AEIS and Schoolpop were to go to court, AEIS could mention the contract said to have a insurance, but since it doesn't mention that 'AEIS shall be named as an additional insured', it would be hard to make it compliant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each policy required hereunder shall provide that AEIS shall receive thirty (30) days advance written notice in the event of a cancellation or material change in such policy. In the event that any service under this Agreement is to be rendered by persons other than Schoolpop's employees, Schoolpop shall furnish AEIS with evidence of insurance for such persons subject to the same terms and conditions as set forth above and applicable to Schoolpop prior to commencement of service by such person(s).
perturbation.justification: The removal of the requirement that AEIS be named as an additional insured in Schoolpop's insurance policies directly contradicts the intention and potentially the requirements of the remaining clauses concerning insurance coverage. While the contradicted text doesn't explicitly state that AEIS needs to be named as an additional insured, the original context (before the omission) made it clear that this was the intention. The omission creates a situation where AEIS may not be adequately protected if a claim arises, directly impacting the enforceability and purpose of the insurance provisions. Without being named as an additional insured, AEIS's ability to directly file a claim or receive direct benefits from Schoolpop's insurance policy is significantly diminished, creating an internal inconsistency within the agreement regarding the level of protection afforded to AEIS.
